ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper presents a notable and coherent new approach: end-to-end optimization of a nonlinear transform coding pipeline using cascaded GDN/IGDN transforms (Sec. 2) together with a continuous relaxation of quantization via additive uniform noise to enable SGD (Sec. 3). It clearly differentiates itself from standard linear transform coding and from related generative/VAE work by addressing discrete quantization and by integrating an entropy code to report actual bit rates (Secs. 3.1, 4). These techniques constitute a non-trivial extension of prior methods (e.g., joint optimized nonlinear transforms and practical coding) and would be informative to experts, though they build on and combine several existing ideas rather than introducing an entirely unprecedented paradigm.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized and follows a logical flow (Introduction → model description → optimization → experiments → discussion → appendix), and the main contributions are stated early (end-to-end optimization of nonlinear transform coding with GDN/IGDN and a continuous relaxation for quantization). Notation is generally consistent and equations are well defined (e.g., (1)–(16), loss (11)), and the appendix explains architectural and optimization details. Figures and example images are frequently referenced and appear to support claims, though the narrative sometimes leans heavily on those figures/appendix (making some dense sections harder to follow without them), and a few paragraphs are jargon-dense and could be streamlined for readability.

SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The main claims are supported: the additive-uniform relaxation and continuous loss (Sec. 3, eqs. (10),(11)) are correctly derived and plausibly approximate quantization (authors acknowledge and empirically evaluate bias in Sec. 4), and the VAE-style interpretation (Sec. 3.1) is mathematically consistent. Experiments compare to JPEG/JPEG2000 with an implemented entropy coder and include RD curves, PSNR and MS-SSIM on standard test sets (Sec. 4 and Appendix 6.2/6.3), supporting the conclusions. Minor technical caveats remain: the model assumes independent marginals in code space and uses only limited spatial context adaptation in the entropy coder (App. 6.2), and separate models are trained per λ; no formal statistical tests are reported. These issues do not overturn the main technical claims, but justify a minor reservation.
